details: Projecting 2,000+ new-hire pilots in 2026. Multiple class dates per month. Aviate program pipeline producing steady flow of candidates. Fleet expansion with 737 MAX and A321neo deliveries driving demand.

Steady new-hire classes averaging 40-45 per month. Strong preference for candidates with 121 experience or military background. Propel program graduates beginning to reach mainline. Over 4,000 pilots eligible to retire by 2029.
Hiring through Envoy/PSA/Piedmont flow-through and direct-entry. Cadet Academy pipeline active. Expect 1,500+ new hires in 2026 as retirements accelerate.

Smaller class sizes than 2024-2025 peak. Fleet growth slowing with MAX delivery delays and network restructuring. Still hiring but more selective on experience minimums. Destination 225 program graduates getting interviews.

Post-Hawaiian Airlines merger integration driving increased pilot demand. Expanding West Coast and Hawaii network. Ascend Pilot Academy partnership filling pipeline. New bases opening as route network grows.
Hiring at reduced pace following failed Spirit merger and network rightsizing. Gateway Select program still active for low-time pilots. Focus on Northeast and Caribbean routes.
Selective hiring as ULCC segment faces margin pressure. A320neo deliveries supporting modest growth. Competitive pay rates after recent contract improvements.
Pilot hiring paused during Alaska Airlines merger integration. Existing Hawaiian pilots being integrated into combined seniority list. Certificate expected to operate independently through 2027.
Largest regional carrier, urgently hiring. Captain upgrade in 2-3 years. Retention bonuses up to $25K. SkyWest cadet program active. Flies for United, Delta, American, and Alaska.
LIFT Academy ab-initio pipeline producing new pilots. Flow-through agreements to United and Delta. Competitive captain pay exceeding $150/hr. Captain upgrade timeline around 2 years.
Delta wholly-owned regional. Guaranteed interview flow-through to Delta mainline. First-year pay package over $95K. Captain upgrade in 18-24 months at most bases.
American Airlines wholly-owned. Flow-through to mainline AA guaranteed for all new hires. First-year compensation exceeding $100K with bonuses. E175 fleet.
American Airlines wholly-owned. CRJ-900 fleet. Guaranteed flow-through to AA mainline. Cadet program available for low-time pilots building hours. Signing bonuses available.
American Airlines wholly-owned. Dash 8 Q-300/Q-400 turboprop fleet. Flow-through to AA. Smaller bases offering quick upgrade to captain. Good option for pilots wanting turboprop PIC time.
Operating as United Express. Restructured after 2023 challenges. Competitive pay after contract improvements. Smaller operation but rapid upgrade times. Hiring bonuses offered.
Operating as American Eagle. Smaller fleet with CRJ-200s. Quick captain upgrade available. Niche operation with East Coast focus. Hiring at reduced pace as fleet stabilizes.
Alaska Airlines wholly-owned. Transitioning to all-E175 fleet. Guaranteed flow to Alaska mainline. Pacific Northwest lifestyle bases. Hiring steadily to support fleet transition.

Selective hiring with strong preference for heavy jet PIC time or military widebody experience. Minimum 1,000 hours PIC turbine preferred. Top-tier compensation in the industry. Memphis domicile for most new hires.
Retirement wave driving consistent new-hire classes through 2027. Over 1,200 pilots eligible to retire in the next 3 years. 747 and 767 fleet. Top compensation package in the industry.

Operating 747s and 767s for Amazon, DHL, and military contracts. CASS and ATAP contracts keeping fleet busy. Hiring selectively for 767 FO positions. Unique lifestyle with long-haul international flying.
Operating 767 freighters primarily for Amazon Prime Air. Small but stable operation. DHL feeder work as well. Hiring a few classes per year to cover attrition and retirement.

Strong demand for fractional flying from high-net-worth clients. 7/7 and 8/6 schedules. ATP required. Fleet includes Citation, Challenger, and Global types. Industry-leading fractional pay after 2024 contract. Columbus domicile but live anywhere.
Growing fractional fleet with Praetor 500/600 and Challenger 350 aircraft. Competitive pay and schedules. LearJet phaseout complete, modern fleet. Multiple crew bases nationwide.

Restructured operations after 2023-2024 financial challenges. Smaller fleet focused on light and midsize jets. Hiring selectively as business stabilizes under Delta partnership. King Air and Citation fleet.
